requirements.summary: Audit and fix all bugs in the video call feature end-to-end, ensuring the video call button correctly opens the VideoCallModal, the useVideoCall hook successfully initialises local media streams, mute and camera controls work correctly, call ending releases all resources cleanly, and the simulated call state transitions work as intended with no console errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the video call button on a friend list item opens the VideoCallModal without errors.
Clicking the video call button in the private chat header opens the VideoCallModal without errors.
The local camera stream initialises successfully and the local video feed is visible in the picture-in-picture area.
The mute button toggles the audio track on/off and its icon/label reflects the current muted state.
The camera toggle button enables/disables the video track and its icon/label reflects the current camera-off state.
The simulated 'Calling…' state transitions to 'Connected' after the expected delay.
Clicking 'End Call' stops all media tracks, releases the media stream, and closes the VideoCallModal.
No unhandled promise rejections or JavaScript console errors appear during the full call lifecycle.
The VideoCallModal UI matches the existing warm coral/amber theme.

file_operations.description: Fix the useVideoCall hook to properly request and initialise the local camera/microphone media stream using getUserMedia, handle promise rejections gracefully, ensure mute and camera toggle controls correctly enable/disable the corresponding media tracks and reflect accurate state, properly stop all media tracks and release the stream when ending the call, and implement the simulated 'Calling…' to 'Connected' state transition with proper cleanup on unmount.

Fix the VideoCallModal to correctly wire the local video stream to the picture-in-picture video element using the videoRef from useVideoCall, ensure mute and camera toggle buttons call the appropriate hook functions and display correct icon/label states, properly invoke endCall to stop media and close the modal, ensure the simulated call state UI displays 'Calling…' then transitions to 'Connected', handle errors from the useVideoCall hook gracefully with user-friendly messages, and verify the modal styling matches the existing warm coral/amber theme using the video-call CSS custom properties.

Ensure the video call button in FriendListItem correctly triggers the onVideoCall callback with the friend's Principal when clicked, verify the button is properly wired to open the VideoCallModal in ChatView, and confirm the button appears on hover with correct styling and accessibility attributes.

Ensure the video call button in the PrivateChatView header correctly triggers the onVideoCall callback with the friend's Principal when clicked, verify the button is properly wired to open the VideoCallModal in ChatView, and confirm the button styling matches the existing UI theme.

Verify that ChatView correctly manages the video call modal state, passes the selected friend's Principal to VideoCallModal when opened, handles the onVideoCall callback from both FriendListItem and PrivateChatView, and properly closes the modal when the call ends or the user dismisses it.
